function.name: read
function.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files only. Images are not supported. For text files, output is truncated to ${DEFAULT_MAX_LINES} lines or ${DEFAULT_MAX_BYTES / 1024}KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

function.name: bash
function.description: Execute a bash command in the current working directory. Returns stdout and stderr. Output is truncated to last ${DEFAULT_MAX_LINES} lines or ${DEFAULT_MAX_BYTES / 1024}KB (whichever is hit first). If truncated, full output is saved to a temp file. Optionally provide a timeout in seconds.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, no default timeout)

function.name: write
function.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file
